samples.xml_description:
<module id="div_16bit">
    <description>Design an 8-bit divider using a combination of sequential and combinational logic.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="rst">Reset signal for initializing the module.</input>
        <input id="sign">1-bit flag indicating whether the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0).</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input for the dividend.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input for the divisor.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">Valid operation signal to initiate the division.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">Signal indicating that the 16-bit result is ready to be consumed.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">Output signal indicating the result is valid and ready to be consumed.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit output containing the result of the division, including the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="IDLE">0</parameter>
        <parameter id="SUB">1</parameter>
        <parameter id="MULT_SEL">2</parameter>
        <parameter id="MULT_1">3</parameter>
        <parameter id="MULT_2">4</parameter>
        <parameter id="MULT_3">5</parameter>
        <parameter id="MULT_4">6</parameter>
        <parameter id="MULT_5">7</parameter>
        <parameter id="SHIFT">8</parameter>
        <parameter id="DONE">9</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <parameter id="IDLE">0</parameter>
        <parameter id="SUB">1</parameter>
        <parameter id="MULT_SEL">2</parameter>
        <parameter id="MULT_1">3</parameter>
        <parameter id="MULT_2">4</parameter>
        <parameter id="MULT_3">5</parameter>
        <parameter id="MULT_4">6</parameter>
        <parameter id="MULT_5">7</parameter>
        <parameter id="SHIFT">8</parameter>
        <parameter id="DONE">9</parameter>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The module operates in a finite state machine (FSM) manner, transitioning through various states to perform the division operation. 
    In the IDLE state, it waits for a valid operation signal (opn_valid) to begin the division process. It saves the input dividend and divisor values.
    It initializes the shift register (SR) with the absolute value of the dividend, shifted left by one bit, and sets the negative divisor (NEG_DIVISOR) to the absolute value of the divisor.
    It sets the counter (cnt) to 1 and start_cnt to 1 to begin the division process.
    In the SUB state, it performs the subtraction of NEG_DIVISOR from the shift register (SR).
    In the MULT_SEL state, it determines whether to perform a multiplication operation based on the carry-out (COUT) from the subtraction.
    In the MULT states (MULT_1 to MULT_5), it performs a series of multiplication operations using a multiplier.
    In the SHIFT state, it shifts the contents of the shift register (SR) and increments the counter (cnt) to continue the division process.
    In the DONE state, it sets the result output to the remainder and quotient values and resets the start_cnt to 0.
    The res_valid signal indicates the result is valid and ready to be consumed.
    </implementation>
</module>
